lines.gurmukhi: ਲੰਗਰੁ ਚਲੈ ਗੁਰ ਸਬਦਿ ਹਰਿ ਤੋਟਿ ਨ ਆਵੀ ਖਟੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: langar chalai gur sabad har tott na aavee khatteeai |
lines.english: The Langar - the Kitchen of the Guru's Shabad has been opened, and its supplies never run short.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੋਵੈ ਸਿਫਤਿ ਖਸੰਮ ਦੀ ਨੂਰੁ ਅਰਸਹੁ ਕੁਰਸਹੁ ਝਟੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: hovai sifat khasam dee noor arasahu kurasahu jhatteeai |
lines.english: The Praises of the Master were sung, and the Divine Light descended from the heavens to the earth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਰਚੇ ਦਿਤਿ ਖਸੰਮ ਦੀ ਆਪ ਖਹਦੀ ਖੈਰਿ ਦਬਟੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: kharache dit khasam dee aap khahadee khair dabatteeai |
lines.english: Whatever His Master gave, He spent; He distributed it all to be eaten.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੁ ਜਿ ਗੁਰਿ ਫੁਰਮਾਇਆ ਕਿਉ ਏਦੂ ਬੋਲਹੁ ਹਟੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: sach ji gur furamaaeaa kiau edoo bolahu hatteeai |
lines.english: The Guru gave the True Command; why should we hesitate to proclaim this?

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਧੁ ਡਿਠੇ ਸਚੇ ਪਾਤਿਸਾਹ ਮਲੁ ਜਨਮ ਜਨਮ ਦੀ ਕਟੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: tudh dditthe sache paatisaah mal janam janam dee katteeai |
lines.english: Gazing upon You, O True King, the filth of countless past lives is washed away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੁਤ੍ਰੀ ਕਉਲੁ ਨ ਪਾਲਿਓ ਕਰਿ ਪੀਰਹੁ ਕੰਨੑ ਮੁਰਟੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: putree kaul na paalio kar peerahu kana muratteeai |
lines.english: His sons did not obey His Word; they turned their backs on Him as Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਿਲਿ ਖੋਟੈ ਆਕੀ ਫਿਰਨੑਿ ਬੰਨੑਿ ਭਾਰੁ ਉਚਾਇਨੑਿ ਛਟੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: dil khottai aakee firana bana bhaar uchaaeina chhatteeai |
lines.english: These evil-hearted ones became rebellious; they carry loads of sin on their backs.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਆਖੀ ਸੋਈ ਕਰੇ ਜਿਨਿ ਕੀਤੀ ਤਿਨੈ ਥਟੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: jin aakhee soee kare jin keetee tinai thatteeai |
lines.english: Whatever the Guru said, Lehna did, and so he was installed on the throne.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਉਣੁ ਹਾਰੇ ਕਿਨਿ ਉਵਟੀਐ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: kaun haare kin uvatteeai |2|
lines.english: Who has lost, and who has won? ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਕੀਤੀ ਸੋ ਮੰਨਣਾ ਕੋ ਸਾਲੁ ਜਿਵਾਹੇ ਸਾਲੀ ॥
lines.translit: jin keetee so mananaa ko saal jivaahe saalee |
lines.english: He who did the work, is accepted as Guru; so which is better - the thistle or the rice?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਆਖੈ ਸਚਾ ਕਰੇ ਸਾ ਬਾਤ ਹੋਵੈ ਦਰਹਾਲੀ ॥
lines.translit: satigur aakhai sachaa kare saa baat hovai darahaalee |
lines.english: Whatever the True Guru says, the True Lord does; it comes to pass instantaneously.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਰਮ ਰਾਇ ਹੈ ਦੇਵਤਾ ਲੈ ਗਲਾ ਕਰੇ ਦਲਾਲੀ ॥
lines.translit: dharam raae hai devataa lai galaa kare dalaalee |
lines.english: The Righteous Judge of Dharma considered the arguments and made the decision.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕੁ ਕਾਇਆ ਪਲਟੁ ਕਰਿ ਮਲਿ ਤਖਤੁ ਬੈਠਾ ਸੈ ਡਾਲੀ ॥
lines.translit: naanak kaaeaa palatt kar mal takhat baitthaa sai ddaalee |
lines.english: Nanak merely changed his body; He still sits on the throne, with hundreds of branches reaching out.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਅੰਗਦ ਦੀ ਦੋਹੀ ਫਿਰੀ ਸਚੁ ਕਰਤੈ ਬੰਧਿ ਬਹਾਲੀ ॥
lines.translit: gur angad dee dohee firee sach karatai bandh bahaalee |
lines.english: Guru Angad was proclaimed, and the True Creator confirmed it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਰਿ ਦਰਵੇਸੁ ਖਸੰਮ ਦੈ ਨਾਇ ਸਚੈ ਬਾਣੀ ਲਾਲੀ ॥
lines.translit: dar daraves khasam dai naae sachai baanee laalee |
lines.english: He is the Dervish - the Saint, at the door of His Lord and Master; He loves the True Name, and the Bani of the Guru's Word.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਰੁ ਸੇਵੇ ਉਮਤਿ ਖੜੀ ਮਸਕਲੈ ਹੋਇ ਜੰਗਾਲੀ ॥
lines.translit: dar seve umat kharree masakalai hoe jangaalee |
lines.english: Standing at His door, His followers serve Him; by this service, their rust is scraped off.

lines.gurmukhi: ਲੰਗਰਿ ਦਉਲਤਿ ਵੰਡੀਐ ਰਸੁ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਖੀਰਿ ਘਿਆਲੀ ॥
lines.translit: langar daulat vanddeeai ras amrit kheer ghiaalee |
lines.english: She distributes the bounty of the Guru's Langar; the kheer - the rice pudding and ghee, is like sweet ambrosia.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਲਵੰਡ ਖੀਵੀ ਨੇਕ ਜਨ ਜਿਸੁ ਬਹੁਤੀ ਛਾਉ ਪਤ੍ਰਾਲੀ ॥
lines.translit: balavandd kheevee nek jan jis bahutee chhaau patraalee |
lines.english: Balwand says that Khivi, the Guru's wife, is a noble woman, who gives soothing, leafy shade to all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਏ ਕਬੂਲੁ ਖਸੰਮ ਨਾਲਿ ਜਾਂ ਘਾਲ ਮਰਦੀ ਘਾਲੀ ॥
lines.translit: pe kabool khasam naal jaan ghaal maradee ghaalee |
lines.english: The Master gave His approval, when Angad exerted Himself heroically.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਸਿਖਾ ਕੇ ਮੁਖ ਉਜਲੇ ਮਨਮੁਖ ਥੀਏ ਪਰਾਲੀ ॥
lines.translit: gurasikhaa ke mukh ujale manamukh thee paraalee |
lines.english: The faces of the Guru's Sikhs are radiant and bright; the self-willed manmukhs are pale, like straw.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੋਰਿਂਓ ਗੰਗ ਵਹਾਈਐ ਦੁਨਿਆਈ ਆਖੈ ਕਿ ਕਿਓਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: horino gang vahaaeeai duniaaee aakhai ki kion |
lines.english: It is as if the Guru made the Ganges flow in the opposite direction, and the world wonders: what has he done?

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਤਾ ਖੀਵੀ ਸਹੁ ਸੋਇ ਜਿਨਿ ਗੋਇ ਉਠਾਲੀ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: maataa kheevee sahu soe jin goe utthaalee |3|
lines.english: Such is the Husband of mother Khivi; He sustains the world. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਈਸਰਿ ਜਗਨਾਥਿ ਉਚਹਦੀ ਵੈਣੁ ਵਿਰਿਕਿਓਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: naanak eesar jaganaath uchahadee vain virikion |
lines.english: Nanak, the Lord, the Lord of the World, spoke the words out loud.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਧਾਣਾ ਪਰਬਤੁ ਕਰਿ ਨੇਤ੍ਰਿ ਬਾਸਕੁ ਸਬਦਿ ਰਿੜਕਿਓਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: maadhaanaa parabat kar netr baasak sabad rirrakion |
lines.english: Making the mountain his churning stick, and the snake-king his churning string, He has churned the Word of the Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਉਦਹ ਰਤਨ ਨਿਕਾਲਿਅਨੁ ਕਰਿ ਆਵਾ ਗਉਣੁ ਚਿਲਕਿਓਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: chaudah ratan nikaalian kar aavaa gaun chilakion |
lines.english: From it, He extracted the fourteen jewels, and illuminated the world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਹਣੇ ਧਰਿਓਨੁ ਛਤ੍ਰੁ ਸਿਰਿ ਅਸਮਾਨਿ ਕਿਆੜਾ ਛਿਕਿਓਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: lahane dharion chhatru sir asamaan kiaarraa chhikion |
lines.english: He raised the royal canopy to wave over the head of Lehna, and raised His glory to the skies.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਅਹਿ ਵੇਖਾਲੀਅਨੁ ਜਿਣਿ ਐਵਡ ਪਿਡ ਠਿਣਕਿਓਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: kudarat eh vekhaaleean jin aivadd pidd tthinakion |
lines.english: He revealed such creative power, and touched such greatness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਿਖਾਂ ਪੁਤ੍ਰਾਂ ਘੋਖਿ ਕੈ ਸਭ ਉਮਤਿ ਵੇਖਹੁ ਜਿ ਕਿਓਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: sikhaan putraan ghokh kai sabh umat vekhahu ji kion |
lines.english: Guru Nanak tested His Sikhs and His sons, and everyone saw what happened.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋਤਿ ਸਮਾਣੀ ਜੋਤਿ ਮਾਹਿ ਆਪੁ ਆਪੈ ਸੇਤੀ ਮਿਕਿਓਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: jot samaanee jot maeh aap aapai setee mikion |
lines.english: His Light merged into the Light, and He blended Him into Himself.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਂ ਸੁਧੋਸੁ ਤਾਂ ਲਹਣਾ ਟਿਕਿਓਨੁ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: jaan sudhos taan lahanaa ttikion |4|
lines.english: When Lehna alone was found to be pure, then He was set on the throne. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫੇਰਿ ਵਸਾਇਆ ਫੇਰੁਆਣਿ ਸਤਿਗੁਰਿ ਖਾਡੂਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: fer vasaaeaa feruaan satigur khaaddoor |
lines.english: Then, the True Guru, the son of Pheru, came to dwell at Khadoor.

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਬੁ ਵਿਣਾਹੇ ਮਾਣਸਾ ਜਿਉ ਪਾਣੀ ਬੂਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: lab vinaahe maanasaa jiau paanee boor |
lines.english: Greed ruins mankind, like the green algae in the water.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਰ੍ਹਿਐ ਦਰਗਹ ਗੁਰੂ ਕੀ ਕੁਦਰਤੀ ਨੂਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: varhiai daragah guroo kee kudaratee noor |
lines.english: In the Guru's Court, the Divine Light shines in its creative power.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਪੁ ਤਪੁ ਸੰਜਮੁ ਨਾਲਿ ਤੁਧੁ ਹੋਰੁ ਮੁਚੁ ਗਰੂਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: jap tap sanjam naal tudh hor much garoor |
lines.english: Meditation, austerities and self-discipline rest with You, while the others are filled with excessive pride.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਉ ਨਿਧਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਨਿਧਾਨੁ ਹੈ ਤੁਧੁ ਵਿਚਿ ਭਰਪੂਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: nau nidh naam nidhaan hai tudh vich bharapoor |
lines.english: You are overflowing with the nine treasures, and the treasure of the Naam, the Name of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਤੁ ਸੁ ਹਾਥ ਨ ਲਭਈ ਤੂੰ ਓਹੁ ਠਰੂਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: jit su haath na labhee toon ohu ttharoor |
lines.english: You are the cooling peace, whose depth cannot be found.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫੇਰਿ ਵਸਾਇਆ ਫੇਰੁਆਣਿ ਸਤਿਗੁਰਿ ਖਾਡੂਰੁ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: fer vasaaeaa feruaan satigur khaaddoor |5|
lines.english: Then the True Guru, the son of Pheru, came to dwell at Khadoor. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨੇੜੈ ਦਿਸੈ ਮਾਤ ਲੋਕ ਤੁਧੁ ਸੁਝੈ ਦੂਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: nerrai disai maat lok tudh sujhai door |
lines.english: People of the world can see only what is near at hand, but You can see far beyond.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿੰਦਾ ਤੇਰੀ ਜੋ ਕਰੇ ਸੋ ਵੰਞੈ ਚੂਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: nindaa teree jo kare so vanyai choor |
lines.english: Whoever slanders You will be totally ruined and destroyed.
